title: PTD Today — Media
disclaimer: Informational only — AI-generated summaries; may contain errors. Verify with the original video.

items.title: Would you go to an in-person dating app event?

ai.summary: Based on the available description, dating app Hinge is expanding its One More Hour social impact initiative with a $1 million fund to support social groups. The fund will back free or affordable in-person social events for young people in New York, Los Angeles and London amid concerns about Gen Z loneliness.
bullets: Gen Z is increasingly struggling with loneliness, per the description.
Hinge announced a $1 million fund to support social groups.
Target cities for the fund are New York, Los Angeles and London.
Funds are intended for free or affordable in-person social events for young people.
This move is described as a global expansion of the One More Hour initiative.
takeaways: Hinge’s targeted funding in NY, LA and London could lead local stakeholders to see more concentrated in-person events—monitor for shifts in demand patterns.
Event-driven gatherings for young people may change timing and location of connectivity and power needs; operators should watch for emerging trends.
Flexible, short‑term capacity planning (e.g., evenings/weekends) may be more relevant than assuming steady usage patterns.
Engaging with local event organizers and venues could help anticipate and respond to episodic infrastructure or service needs.

items.title: Build Knowledge Graph-powered AI Agents For Your Use Case | Databases for AI

ai.summary: Based on the available description… This AWS Events session demonstrates how to automatically build a knowledge graph from raw documents and connect it to an AI agent so the agent can understand your specific business. The end-to-end demo emphasizes no graph expertise is required.
bullets: Problem: general AI agents lack company-specific knowledge.
Solution: construct a knowledge graph from raw documents to encode business knowledge.
Demo: end-to-end build of the graph and connection to an AI agent.
No graph-query or graph-theory skills required to follow the workflow.
References a prior episode comparing VectorDB vs GraphDB for Gen AI agents.
takeaways: Enables AI agents to access organization-specific knowledge rather than generic models.
Automating ingestion from documents can make operational and documentation data more usable by AI.
Lower barrier to adoption since specialized graph expertise isn’t required.
An end-to-end approach can accelerate pilots that integrate AI agents with internal knowledge.

items.title: Leveraging tax credits and incentives for sustainability projects

ai.summary: Based on the available description, Randa Barsoum of PwC US explains that while energy incentives are commonly associated with sustainability projects, other tax credits and incentives—such as state and local abatements, location-based incentives, and R&D tax credits tied to innovation—also matter. The central message is that viewing sustainability investments through a broader tax lens can unlock meaningful tax value for businesses.
bullets: Energy incentives are familiar but represent only part of available tax support.
State and local abatements can materially support sustainability projects.
Location-based incentives should factor into site and investment decisions.
R&D tax credits can apply to innovation efforts like reimagining packaging or processes.
A broader tax perspective can increase the value of sustainability investments.
takeaways: Don’t rely solely on energy incentives—inventory other credit and incentive types when planning projects.
Assess state and local abatements as part of site selection and tax planning for new builds or expansions.
Factor location-based incentives into decisions about where to place grid or data center assets.
Explore R&D tax credits for sustainability-related innovation in processes or designs to capture additional tax value.

items.title: Copilot helps Christa find her inner star 🌟

ai.summary: Based on the available description… The video frames Copilot as a supportive tool that helps Christa "find the star within," using an uplifting, starry metaphor. Viewers are invited to download and try Copilot via the link in the bio.
bullets: No transcript available; description is brief and metaphorical.
Theme: finding an "inner star" with Copilot's help.
Tone: uplifting/encouraging (star emoji used).
Clear call-to-action: download and try Copilot via link in bio.
takeaways: Copilot is promoted as a downloadable end-user tool—expect potential user-driven demand for access and onboarding.
Organizations should decide on access and policy for Copilot usage since it’s being marketed directly to users.
IT/support teams may need to prepare for user questions or basic deployment assistance if adoption grows.
Monitor adoption signals to inform capacity, authentication, and licensing planning.

items.title: Bringing In $251K A Year With 3 Jobs In The Bay Area

ai.summary: Based on the available description, Naseema McElroy, 44, is a labor and delivery nurse in Brentwood, California who also runs a personal finance brand called Financially Intentional. She accumulated nearly $1 million in debt in 2015, documented her debt-payoff journey, became debt-free by 2017, and now brings in $251K a year working three jobs in the Bay Area.
bullets: Labor and delivery nurse and founder of the Financially Intentional brand.
Accumulated nearly $1 million in debt in 2015 and documented her payoff journey.
Was debt-free by 2017 after paying down that debt.
Now earns $251K annually through three jobs in the Bay Area.
Profiled in CNBC Make It's Millennial Money series.
takeaways: A single individual combining in-person work (nursing) and a digital business highlights reliance on consistent power and connectivity for both clinical and online activities.
Multiple income streams from the same person (three jobs plus a brand) suggest demand for reliable, always-on infrastructure to support distributed work schedules.
High earned income concentrated in the Bay Area signals a user base that may expect robust uptime and low-latency services from local data centers and grid operators.

items.title: GeForce Garage - Summer of RTX Community Build

ai.summary: Based on the available description… This GeForce Garage video announces the winner of NVIDIA’s Summer of RTX r/buildapc Community Build Contest, u/JeffersonPutnam, and showcases their small-form-factor build centered on a GeForce RTX 5090 Founders Edition. The description lists the full system specs, links to the PCPartPicker build, and notes GeForce host Dwight plus NVIDIA GeForce social channels.
bullets: Winner: u/JeffersonPutnam — SFF GeForce RTX 5090 rig showcased.
Chassis: Fractal Design Era 2; GPU: GeForce RTX 5090 Founders Edition.
Key components: MSI MPG B850I Edge, Ryzen 7 9800X3D, 32GB DDR5-6000, Corsair SF1000 PSU, Corsair Nautilus 240 RS AIO.
PCPartPicker link provided for the complete parts list.
Video credits GeForce host Dwight and links to NVIDIA GeForce social channels.
takeaways: Flagship desktop GPUs like the RTX 5090 can be integrated into compact SFF builds but require careful power planning—this build pairs the card with a 1000W SFX PSU (Corsair SF1000).
Effective cooling is critical in confined spaces—this build uses a Corsair Nautilus 240 RS AIO, indicating liquid cooling as a practical SFF solution for high-power components.
Motherboard and chassis choices (MSI MPG B850I Edge + Fractal Era 2) highlight the need to verify component fit, thermals, and I/O when deploying powerful GPUs in space-constrained systems.
For procurement and deployment, ensure PSU form-factor compatibility, sufficient power headroom, and appropriate cooling solutions when considering desktop-class GPUs for edge or workstation roles.

items.title: TTEC accelerates AI deployment and revenue streams with Google Cloud Consulting

ai.summary: Based on the available description… TTEC partnered with Google Cloud Consulting to rapidly prototype and deploy GenAI solutions, unlocking new revenue streams. The engagement used Google Cloud's infrastructure to streamline operations, boost developer productivity, and compress TTEC's digital transformation timeline.
bullets: TTEC collaborated with Google Cloud Consulting to accelerate AI prototyping and deployment.
GenAI and Google Cloud infrastructure were used to streamline operations and improve developer productivity.
The project unlocked entirely new revenue streams for TTEC.
Google Cloud Consulting helped compress the digital transformation timeline.
The partnership expanded the company’s ambitions for AI-driven customer experience solutions.
takeaways: Cloud consulting can shorten AI deployment timelines, reducing internal resource strain for data center teams.
Leveraging managed cloud infrastructure supports faster developer productivity gains and time-to-market for AI services.
Rapid prototyping via cloud partners can accelerate monetization of AI capabilities and create new revenue streams.
Grid and data center decision-makers should evaluate cloud architectures that support GenAI workloads to enable operational efficiency.
